question: Define an `Employee` base class with a `displayInfo()` method. Create a `Manager` class that inherits from `Employee` and overrides `displayInfo()` to include additional manager-specific details.

test_cases.expected:
--- Employee Details ---
Employee Name: Alice Wonderland
Employee ID: EMP-001

--- Manager 1 Details ---
Manager Name: Bob The Builder
Manager ID: MGR-001
Manager Department: Construction (Team Size: 15)
Special Note: Oversees operations.

--- Manager 2 Details ---
Manager Name: Charlie Chaplin
Manager ID: MGR-002
Manager Department: Entertainment (Team Size: 5)
Special Note: Oversees operations.

errors: Global `using namespace std;` pollutes the global namespace, making code less readable and prone to name clashes.
Constructors pass `std::string` parameters by value, leading to unnecessary string copies. Passing by `const std::string&` would be more efficient.
`Manager::displayInfo()` re-implements the base class's display logic instead of calling `Employee::displayInfo()`, violating the DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) principle and making maintenance harder.
Excessive use of `std::endl` which flushes the output buffer on each call, potentially reducing performance. Using `\n` is generally preferred unless an immediate flush is required.

Inefficient string concatenations using `+` operator in `Manager::displayInfo()`, which can create multiple temporary string objects. Using `std::stringstream` or combining output into a single `std::cout` statement would be more efficient.
